title: Pendulum Collision Lab
description: A physics sandbox where a ShaderBall swings on a pendulum chain and collides with a Corset placed on a sliding platform. The user adjusts the pendulum release angle, platform position, and surface friction to observe momentum transfer and predict where the Corset will slide to after impact.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 with importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net. Load ShaderBall.glb and Corset.glb using GLTFLoader. Scene setup: a ground plane with a grid texture, a vertical support frame made of thin BoxGeometry beams, and a pendulum arm (a line of small spheres as a chain) attached to the top crossbar. The ShaderBall hangs at the end of the pendulum chain. The Corset sits on a flat rectangular platform on the ground to the right of the pendulum rest position. Normalize both models to roughly similar visual scale (ShaderBall ~1 unit diameter, Corset ~0.8 units tall). Camera: perspective at position (0, 3, 8) looking at origin. Lighting: one DirectionalLight with shadows enabled, one HemisphereLight for ambient fill. Interactions (5 steps): (1) A slider labeled 'Release Angle' (15-85 degrees) controls how far back the pendulum is pulled. (2) A slider labeled 'Platform Distance' (1-4 units) sets horizontal offset of the Corset platform from pendulum center. (3) A slider labeled 'Friction' (0.0-1.0) controls how quickly the Corset decelerates after being hit. (4) A 'Release' button drops the pendulum; animate the ShaderBall swinging under gravity using simple pendulum equations (theta = theta0 * cos(sqrt(g/L)*t)). When the ShaderBall reaches the lowest point and contacts the Corset, apply a simplified elastic collision transferring velocity to the Corset. (5) The Corset slides along the platform decelerating based on friction, leaving a colored trail showing its path. Display a HUD showing impact velocity, transferred momentum, and final slide distance. Add a 'Reset' button to return everything to initial state. Use OrbitControls for camera. All physics should be manually computed in the animation loop, no external physics engine. Add subtle particle sparks at the collision point using a small BufferGeometry point system.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  pendulumAngle: number,         // current angle of pendulum in degrees from vertical (0 = hanging straight down, positive = pulled back)
  pendulumAngularVelocity: number,         // angular velocity of pendulum in degrees per second
  releaseAngleSetting: number,         // user-set release angle in degrees (15-85)
  platformDistance: number,         // horizontal distance of Corset platform from pendulum center in units (1-4)
  frictionCoefficient: number,         // surface friction coefficient (0.0-1.0) controlling Corset deceleration
  corsetPositionX: number,         // current X position of Corset in world coordinates
  corsetVelocityX: number,         // current horizontal velocity of Corset in units per second
  isPendulumReleased: boolean,         // true when pendulum is actively swinging after release button pressed
  hasCollisionOccurred: boolean,         // true after ShaderBall has made contact with Corset
  corsetFinalPositionX: number,         // final resting X position of Corset after sliding stops
  totalCollisions: number,         // count of collisions between ShaderBall and Corset since page load
}
